title: Aureline benchmark-lab run-result record
description: Boundary schema for one benchmark-lab run-result record. The record pins every result to a single exact-build identity (schemas/build/exact_build_identity.schema.json), a single protected corpus-manifest revision (fixtures/benchmarks/corpus_manifest.yaml), a single fitness-function catalog revision (artifacts/bench/fitness_function_catalog.yaml), and a single hardware-definition ref so later methodology, comparability, and waiver lanes consume one schema family rather than diverging per-lane dialects. Raw trace bodies, raw fixture bytes, raw log bodies, and raw signing material never cross this boundary; the record carries opaque refs, typed vocabulary, integer counters, numeric SLI values, and timestamps only. Adding a new comparability class, quarantine-reason class, measurement-status class, data-source class, row-result class, or trend-direction class is additive-minor and requires a schema-version bump; repurposing an existing value is breaking and requires a new decision row. The record is the single source of truth for nightly raw artifacts (artifacts/benchmarks/dashboard_seed/raw/*.json) and for the machine-readable half of the benchmark-report governance packet family (schemas/governance/governance_packet.schema.json packet_family=benchmark_report).

run_result_schema_version.description: Integer schema version for the benchmark run-result export. Current value is 1. Bumped only on breaking payload changes; additive-optional fields do not bump this version.

record_kind.description: Frozen discriminator. Every run-result record carries record_kind = benchmark_run_result_record so consumers that multiplex several packet families on one parser can route by field.

opaque_id.description: Opaque, stable id. Used for run_id, raw_artifact_ref, trace_bundle_ref, hardware_definition_ref, dashboard_ref, evidence-consumer channels, and waiver ids. No raw URLs, raw file paths outside the repo, raw logs, or raw signing material appear here.

monotonic_timestamp.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp produced from a monotonic clock source.

measured_date.description: YYYY-MM-DD date the run was captured. Mirrors fitness_function_catalog.yaml#packet_export_shape.measured_on so the fitness_function_snapshot block composes against this record without re-deriving the field.

run_context_class.description: Frozen run-context vocabulary. reference_capture is a full nightly lane on the approved hardware baseline — the only class admissible as protected-evidence input to a shiproom packet. provisional_capture marks a row captured before the benchmark council has ratified a threshold or data source; the record is admissible for trend inspection but MUST NOT fail release gates. self_capture is a developer-local or contributor-machine capture; reports MUST mark it distinct from reference_capture in every surface. smoke_subset is the fast subset the nightly lane runs before the full corpus; it is admissible for keeping the lane green but MUST NOT be confused with a reference capture.

comparability_class.description: Frozen comparability vocabulary. comparable_to_baseline means the run matches the benchmark-council-approved hardware definition and the same exact-build-identity axes as the baseline; comparable_to_prior_run_same_host means comparability is only guaranteed to the immediately prior nightly on the same host; not_yet_comparable is the default when a new corpus revision or threshold has just landed; quarantined means the run is excluded from trend comparisons for a named reason.

quarantine_reason_class.description: Frozen vocabulary for why a run is quarantined. Every quarantined run MUST cite at least one reason; every comparable run MUST cite none.

measurement_status_class.description: Frozen measurement-status vocabulary. captured is the normal path. not_measured covers rows the run deliberately did not exercise (e.g. a corpus row outside the smoke subset). skipped_by_gate covers rows the lane skipped because a precondition failed (toolchain missing, hardware mismatch, corpus fixture unresolved). errored covers rows whose measurement itself failed; a row with status errored MUST carry a non-empty error_reason.

row_result_class.description: Frozen row-result vocabulary. Aligned with fitness_function_catalog.yaml#packet_export_shape.fitness_function_snapshot.result. warn is distinct from fail: a warn keeps the lane green but surfaces the regression condition on the dashboard. waived MUST carry at least one waiver_ref. provisional MUST appear only on rows whose fitness-catalog row_status is provisional.

trend_direction_class.description: Frozen trend-direction vocabulary. improving / regressing are set by the dashboard generator against the comparable-prior-run window declared on the run. unchanged is explicit; unknown_insufficient_history is the default until the trend window is populated; unknown_not_comparable is set when comparability_class forbids direct comparison.

threshold_mode_class.description: Frozen threshold-mode vocabulary. MUST match the threshold_modes set in artifacts/bench/fitness_function_catalog.yaml. Rows that carry a threshold_mode of to_be_set_by_benchmark_council MUST NOT fail the run; they may only warn.

sli_kind_class.description: Frozen SLI-kind vocabulary. latency_*_ms values are milliseconds as non-negative numbers. ratio_unit_interval values MUST be in [0, 1]. count_non_negative values are integers >= 0. boolean_pass_fail values resolve to 1.0 for pass and 0.0 for fail on the numeric axis; the corresponding row_result carries the typed verdict. structural_digest rows carry a digest_ref and no numeric value.

data_source_kind_class.description: Frozen data-source-kind vocabulary. MUST match the data_source_kinds set in artifacts/bench/fitness_function_catalog.yaml.

lane_class.description: Producer lane that captured the run. ci_nightly is the protected nightly lane. Reference captures MUST appear only on ci_nightly.

trigger_kind_class.description: Frozen trigger-kind vocabulary. scheduled_nightly is the cron-driven nightly. manual_dispatch covers operator-driven replays. commit_gated is reserved for future per-commit lanes; not yet wired. developer_invocation pairs with lane_class = developer_local.

host_platform_class_token.description: Host-platform class carried on the row. Mirrors fixtures/benchmarks/corpus_manifest.yaml#host_platform_classes. When not host_independent the run MUST carry host_os and rustc_target_triple.

definition_id.description: Opaque id for the hardware-definition row the run matched. The hardware-definition catalog is not yet seeded; this field reserves the identity so later definitions land without renaming.

definition_revision.description: Revision of the hardware-definition row the run matched.

is_council_approved_baseline.description: True when the definition is on the benchmark-council-approved baseline list. Runs with this flag false MUST NOT claim comparability_class = comparable_to_baseline.

notes_ref.description: Opaque ref to a human-readable hardware note (free-form text lives outside this boundary).

hardware_definition_reference.description: Hardware-definition reference. The exact hardware taxonomy (CPU model classes, DRAM tiers, storage classes, thermal envelopes) will land with a later benchmark-council decision; this record reserves the identity shape.

exact_build_identity_ref.description: Opaque pin to schemas/build/exact_build_identity.schema.json#exact_build_identity_ref. Every non-dev run MUST resolve this ref back to the exact-build-identity record captured alongside the binary the run measured.

producer_lane_class.description: Producer lane. Mirrors schemas/build/exact_build_identity.schema.json#producer_lane_class. Reference captures MUST carry ci_nightly / ci_beta / ci_release / ci_hotfix only.

workspace_version.description: Rendered workspace.package version string copied from the exact-build identity.

commit_short_hash.description: Twelve-character lower-case hex prefix of the commit hash, convenience field for dashboard rendering. Diffing uses exact_build_identity_ref, not this field.

build_identity_reference.description: Build-identity reference. The record keys trend comparability off exact_build_identity_ref, not off free-text version labels.

manifest_revision.description: Manifest revision the run exercised. Mirrors fixtures/benchmarks/corpus_manifest.yaml#manifest_revision.

corpus_manifest_reference.description: Corpus-manifest reference. Every run is keyed by the exact manifest revision it exercised; a fitness-function row cited against a different manifest revision is a validation failure.

catalog_revision.description: Catalog revision the run was scored against. Mirrors artifacts/bench/fitness_function_catalog.yaml#catalog_revision.

fitness_function_catalog_reference.description: Fitness-function catalog reference. Every row-result's fitness_row_id resolves to a catalog row at this revision.

toolchain_pin_digest.description: Content digest of rust-toolchain.toml. Used to detect a drifted pin between otherwise-identical runs.

lockfile_digest.description: Content digest of Cargo.lock. Used to detect a dependency-graph drift between otherwise-identical runs.

toolchain_pin_reference.description: Toolchain-pin reference. Carried on the run so the dashboard can surface a toolchain drift as a named regression condition.

waiver_reference_list.description: Ordered list of waiver ids from artifacts/governance/ownership_matrix.yaml#waivers active at run time. Every row_result = waived MUST cite at least one id from this list.

evidence_consumer_channel_list.description: Ordered list of evidence-consumer channels this run feeds. Mirrors fixtures/benchmarks/corpus_manifest.yaml#evidence_consumer_channel_classes and the per-fitness-row evidence_consumer_channels lists.

fitness_row_id.description: Fitness-function row id from artifacts/bench/fitness_function_catalog.yaml#rows[].id. MUST resolve to a row at fitness_function_catalog.catalog_revision.

corpus_refs.description: Ordered list of corpus ids from fixtures/benchmarks/corpus_manifest.yaml#fixtures[].id that this row exercised. Every id MUST resolve against corpus_manifest.manifest_revision; an unresolved id is a validation failure.

threshold_at_measurement.description: Copy of the fitness-catalog threshold at measurement time. Null when the row is counts_only or structural_digest. String literal 'to_be_set_by_benchmark_council' mirrors the provisional placeholder used in artifacts/bench/fitness_function_catalog.yaml.

sample_size.description: Number of samples the SLI was computed over. Rows with sample_size = 0 MUST carry measurement_status = not_measured or skipped_by_gate.

measured_value.description: Typed measured value. Null when measurement_status != captured.

host_os.description: Host OS for host-dependent rows. Null admissible only when host_platform_class = host_independent.

rustc_target_triple.description: rustc target triple for host-dependent rows. Null admissible only when host_platform_class = host_independent.

data_source_ref.description: Repo-relative path or opaque channel id for the evidence body. Null admissible when data_source_kind = to_be_wired_by_benchmark_council.

trace_bundle_ref.description: Opaque ref back to a trace bundle (schemas/traces/spike_timing.schema.json record or bundle). Present when the row was captured from a trace.

expires_on.description: Waiver expiry date. Required when result = waived; null otherwise.

kind.description: Frozen vocabulary for why the row triggered a regression or warn. A row with result = fail or warn MUST carry exactly one regression_trigger_ref.

summary_ref.description: Opaque ref to a human-readable summary line on the dashboard. The summary_ref MUST resolve against a row in the companion human-readable summary.

regression_trigger_ref.description: Regression / warn trigger envelope. Required when result is fail or warn; null otherwise.

error_reason.description: Short string describing why the measurement errored. Required when measurement_status = errored; null otherwise.

notes_ref.description: Opaque ref to supplementary notes on this row (free-form text lives outside this boundary).

row_measurement_record.description: One per-fitness-row measurement. A run-result record carries zero or more row records; a run with zero rows MUST also carry a quarantine reason.

quarantine_reasons.description: Ordered list of quarantine reasons. MUST be empty when comparability_class != quarantined; MUST be non-empty when comparability_class = quarantined.

prior_run_refs.description: Ordered list of prior run_ids the dashboard used to compute trend_direction for rows in this run. MUST be empty when comparability_class = not_yet_comparable or quarantined.

window_span_days.description: Number of days the trend window covers.

trend_window.description: Trend-window envelope. Null when comparability_class = not_yet_comparable or quarantined.

comparability_envelope.description: Comparability envelope. Pins the run's comparability class, quarantine reasons, and trend window so the dashboard does not re-derive them from free-text notes.

row_count_by_result.description: Bucketed row-result counts. The sum of buckets MUST equal the length of rows[].

regression_trigger_count_by_kind.description: Bucketed regression-trigger counts. Every regression_trigger_ref.kind increments exactly one bucket.

human_readable_summary_ref.description: Repo-relative path to the human-readable summary this run produced (e.g. artifacts/benchmarks/dashboard_seed/report/<run_id>.md). The run is non-conforming if this file does not exist in the same artifact set.

raw_artifact_ref.description: Repo-relative path to the raw run-result JSON this record was serialised into (typically artifacts/benchmarks/dashboard_seed/raw/<run_id>.json).

dashboard_ref.description: Repo-relative path to the rolled-up dashboard snapshot (e.g. artifacts/benchmarks/dashboard_seed/dashboard.json). Null when the dashboard snapshot is not yet seeded.

summary_envelope.description: Summary envelope. The machine-readable half of the benchmark-report governance packet reads row_count_by_result and regression_trigger_count_by_kind directly; the human-readable half reads human_readable_summary_ref.

run_id.description: Globally-unique opaque id for this run. Convention: 'benchmark_run.<YYYYMMDD>.<lane>.<short_hash>'. The dashboard rolls up runs by run_id; two runs with the same run_id are a validation failure.

rows.description: Ordered list of per-fitness-row measurements in fitness_function_catalog order. A row may appear at most once per run_id.

notes_ref.description: Opaque ref to run-level notes (free-form text lives outside this boundary).

benchmark_run_result_record.description: One benchmark-lab run-result record. The nightly lane writes exactly one record per run; the dashboard generator reads a directory of records keyed by run_id to produce a rolled-up dashboard snapshot. The record is the single source of truth for evidence lineage: every protected speed / safety claim that cites a benchmark number resolves through (exact_build_identity_ref, corpus_manifest.manifest_revision, fitness_function_catalog.catalog_revision, hardware_definition.definition_id) on one of these records rather than a free-text label.
